steps.title: Use the study result
steps.content: The study collected Western music preference assessments and personality trait reports from participants in fifty-three countries. It found that the five-factor framework captured preferences in Western music and that the correlations between those music factors and personality traits were similar across cultures.

steps.title: Stay within the evidence
steps.content: The conclusion must stay with Western music preferences, personality profiles, and cross-cultural similarity. The passage does not test non-Western music, does not discuss familiarity with Western genres, and does not compare the results with what researchers previously assumed.

steps.title: Reject unsupported extensions
steps.content:
• Choice A jumps to non-Western music styles, which the study did not assess.
  • Choice B invents a comparison to researchers' previous assumptions.
  • Choice C introduces familiarity with Western music, a variable not discussed in the passage.
The answer is D.
